messages.content:
Under feudalism, why did kings give fiefs to their nobles?
In exchange for their families working the land to feed the manor.
In exchange for their travels along the Silk Road.
 
In exchange for them conquering foreign lands.
In exchange for their allegiance to fight on the kings behalf

Under feudalism, kings gave fiefs to their nobles primarily in exchange for their allegiance to fight on the king's behalf. This arrangement ensured that the nobles would provide military support and protection to the king and his kingdom. In feudal society, warfare was a common and constant reality, and the king relied on the loyalty and military abilities of his nobles to maintain his power and defend the realm.

What was the outcome of Charlemagne's military campaigns through Western Europe?
The spread of infectious diseases and a major decline in population resulting in an economic crisis
A divided Europe on the brink of a major war over religious dominance
 
The violent conquering of lands resulting in a united Holy Roman Empire and common Christian faith
A great amassing of wealth through controlled trade routes in the Italian city-states and Mediterranean region

The outcome of Charlemagne's military campaigns through Western Europe was the violent conquering of lands resulting in the formation of the Carolingian Empire, also known as the Holy Roman Empire. Through his military expeditions, Charlemagne expanded his kingdom and established control over a significant portion of Western Europe. However, it is important to note that his campaigns were not solely focused on conquest but also aimed to spread Christianity and consolidate political power. Although there were economic and political benefits to the expansion, such as increased wealth and control over trade routes, the primary outcome was the establishment of a united and powerful empire under Charlemagne's rule.

What 5th century event caused Western Europe to fragment into warring groups?
the assassination of Pope Leo III
the Massacre of Verden
 
the fall of Rome
the death of Charlemagne

The 5th-century event that caused Western Europe to fragment into warring groups was the fall of Rome. The Roman Empire, which had controlled much of Western Europe, began to decline and eventually collapsed in the 5th century. This led to a power vacuum and a period of political instability. With the absence of a central authority, various Germanic tribes and other groups began to assert their own control and compete for territorial dominance. This fragmentation resulted in the emergence of different kingdoms and regional powers, leading to a period of conflict and warfare among these warring groups.

Which of the following best explains Western Europe following Charlemagne's death in 814?
Western Europe moved away from the feudal system although they eventually divided into various nation-states.
Western Europe fell into political turmoil and reverted, again, to the system of feudalism.
 
Western Europe was firmly controlled by Charlemagne's heirs for several generations.
Charlemagne's successors created peaceful alliances with the Vikings and controlled an even larger area.

The best explanation for Western Europe following Charlemagne's death in 814 is that it fell into political turmoil and reverted, again, to the system of feudalism. After Charlemagne's death, his empire was divided among his heirs, resulting in the fragmentation of the Carolingian Empire. This led to a period of political instability and the reemergence of feudalism as the dominant social and political system. The once-unified empire gave way to a decentralized feudal structure, with local lords and nobles asserting control over their territories. This fragmentation and return to feudalism marked a significant shift in the political landscape of Western Europe following Charlemagne's death.

How did the crowning of Charlemagne as Holy Roman Emperor in 800 C.E. strengthen his control over Western Europe?

It gave him a divine right to rule.


It forgave his previous atrocities.

 

It allowed him to annex the remaining Western territories.


It showed his allegiance to the Pope.

The crowning of Charlemagne as Holy Roman Emperor in 800 C.E. strengthened his control over Western Europe in multiple ways. One significant factor was that it showed his allegiance to the Pope. By being crowned by the Pope, it solidified Charlemagne's legitimacy and authority as the temporal ruler of Western Europe, as well as his support from the powerful religious institution. This endorsement provided him with a significant boost in his political and religious legitimacy and solidified his control over the territories he had already conquered and annexed.

However, it is important to note that the notion of a "divine right to rule" was not explicitly associated with his crowning. The concept of divine right evolved later in history and was not a prevailing belief during Charlemagne's time. Additionally, the crowning did not forgive any atrocities committed by Charlemagne, nor did it directly result in the annexation of remaining Western territories.

How did the Carolingian Renaissance impact education?

It was the period when the first printing press was designed, providing books that were affordable to everyone.


It created the first systematic scholarly system, although Charlemagne failed to address the issue of illiteracy.

 

It saw the formation of many monastic schools and many scholars were invited to encourage education.


It neglected it by emphasizing the divine status of Charlemagne.

The Carolingian Renaissance had a significant impact on education in Western Europe. One of the main contributions was the creation of a systematic scholarly system. Charlemagne established a court school at his palace in Aachen and promoted the establishment of monastic and cathedral schools throughout his empire. These schools aimed to educate both clergy and members of the nobility. Charlemagne also invited scholars from various regions to his court, such as Alcuin of York, who played a crucial role in the revival of learning and education.

The establishment of these schools and the promotion of scholarly activities during the Carolingian Renaissance helped to preserve and revive classical learning and knowledge. Manuscripts were copied and preserved in monastic scriptoriums, contributing to the preservation of important works from ancient Greece and Rome.

While the Carolingian Renaissance did not directly address the issue of illiteracy, its focus on education and the promotion of learning played a significant role in shaping the educational landscape of Western Europe and laying the foundation for educational institutions that followed in the Middle Ages. The emphasis on scholarship and learning during this period had a lasting impact on the development of education in Western Europe.
when i give you these anwser and questions keep it short and only the awnser to the question

Understood! I will provide concise answers to your questions.

Q: How did the crowning of Charlemagne as Holy Roman Emperor in 800 C.E. strengthen his control over Western Europe?
A: It showed his allegiance to the Pope.

Q: How did the Carolingian Renaissance impact education?
A: It saw the formation of many monastic schools and invited scholars to encourage education.

Why was Charlemagne crowned with the crown of the Holy Roman Emperor?

Because he threatened the Pope until he got what he wanted.


Because he helped restore order in Rome when the Pope was attacked.

 

Because he helped the Pope in the 787 Battle of Verden.


Because he spread Christianity peacefully among the Saxons.
Charlemagne was crowned with the crown of the Holy Roman Emperor because he helped restore order in Rome when the Pope was attacked.

Which of the following is an assertion of the Divine Right of Kings?
The ruler of Rome has a right to rule the rest of Europe
Monarchs are subject only to the will of the people
 
Attempts to limit a monarch's power violate God's will
A monarch's rulings can only be reversed by the Supreme Court
Attempts to limit a monarch's power violate God's will.

How did Romans 13:1-2 from the Bible impact the rule of Charlemagne?

The verses were used by his opposition, as they state that the Throne of Peter was the supreme authority on Earth.


Charlemagne used these verses to defend the concept of a king being divinely appointed and therefore free from earthly authority.

 

Charlemagne used these verses because they state that a king is bound by earthly institutions and must adhere to civil and divine authority.


The verses were used by his opposition, as they state that no man could have divine power.
Charlemagne used Romans 13:1-2 from the Bible to defend the concept of a king being divinely appointed and therefore free from earthly authority.

Which of the following is FALSE regarding the Song of Roland?

It was written during the lifetime of Charlemagne.


It was a fictionalized poem.

 

It gave Charlemagne a near god status.


It highlighted Charlemagne's rule.

The statement "It was written during the lifetime of Charlemagne" is FALSE regarding the Song of Roland. The Song of Roland was not written during the lifetime of Charlemagne. It is believed to have been composed in the 11th or 12th century, long after Charlemagne's death.

This unit of structure is a small piece of the overall story. You can tell when it changes because there's a different set of characters on stage or the location depicted on stage has changed.

the prologue


the climax


the acts

 

the epilogue


the scenes
The unit of structure described is the scenes.

This is the term for the large sections of a play. Some plays only need one section, but others need as many as five (or even more).

the prologue


the climax


the scenes

 

the epilogue


the acts
The term for the large sections of a play is the acts.

Which part of the play happens before the story starts?

the climax


the acts


the prologue

 

the scenes


the epilogue
The part of the play that happens before the story starts is the prologue.

Which part of the play comes at the end, after the main problem in the play has been solved?

the acts


the prologue


the scenes

 

the climax


the epilogue
The part of the play that comes at the end, after the main problem in the play has been solved, is the epilogue.

Using the football analogy, which part of the play is like the post-game show?

the acts


the epilogue


the scenes

 

the climax


the prologue

In the football analogy, the post-game show would be akin to the epilogue in a play. Just as the epilogue provides a wrap-up and reflection on the events of the play, the post-game show in football typically involves analysis, highlights, and discussions about the outcome and key moments of the game.

What the characters say is one major part of a script. What is the term for what the characters say?

lines


stage directions


setting

 

dramatic foil


tone
The term for what the characters say in a script is "lines."

If a character has to move both downstage and stage left until he reaches the edge of the stage, where will he end up?

in the middle of the stage


at the back, left hand corner of the stage


at the front, right hand corner of the stage

 

at the front, left hand corner of the stage


at the back, right hand corner of the stage
If a character has to move both downstage and stage left until he reaches the edge of the stage, they will end up at the front, left-hand corner of the stage.

Stage directions aren't only directions about where the actors should move; they're also directions about....

publication of the play


whether to go stage left or stage right


how actors should deliver their lines

 

copyright


the playwright

Stage directions are also directions about how actors should deliver their lines. They provide guidance on the tone, volume, pace, and other aspects of the delivery that help convey the intended meaning and emotions of the script.

If the stage directions instruct an actor to walk upstage, which choice describes where the actor will walk?

towards the right side of the stage


towards the back of the stage


towards the front of the stage

 

towards the middle of the stage


towards the left side of the stage
If the stage directions instruct an actor to walk upstage, the actor will walk towards the back of the stage.

Which of the following choices is probably a line from a play?

Bess walks upstage, humming softly.


Bess moves quickly to center stage.


Bess, smiling at the audience, kneels on the apron, stage left.

 

Bess: You're my hero!


Bess is a cheerful school teacher from a small town who looks at the world with youthful optimism.
The line from a play would be "Bess: You're my hero!"
